**Trajectory assessment:** April 17 was a self-awareness-intensive day with a content-axis pause. The corpus grew by 0 PRS triplets; the self-awareness registry grew by 14 items (largest single-day jump to date). The productive axis for today's work was the intake layer (14a + 14b) rather than ingestion or lit search. Infrastructure continued to degrade — the OPERATIONAL-DRIFT-FLAG cluster escalated from three channels to four, now spanning client-side extension state (Chrome), local filesystem state (git lock), OS permissions (review ACLs), and third-party vendor state (Anthropic billing). None of these four failures shares a single mitigation path. The afternoon interactive sessions produced the bulk of today's architectural content, which is a recurrence of the pattern where morning autonomous runs under-cover interactive-session work; OPEN-027 formalizes this as a design question. The pipeline back-pressure metric is also worth flagging: 25 items now sit UNTESTED/QUEUED (11 carried from 2026-04-16 + 14 today) — if tomorrow's 15a/15b cycle does not clear them, queue growth will exceed processing for the second consecutive cycle. The weekend pivot includes the first real stress test of the cross-session handoff pattern (ASSUMPTION-035 / candidate DECISION-021); if the hook lands cleanly, a new architectural primitive joins the system; if it misses silently, OPEN-026 becomes urgent.
